details: Checking for structural contradictions across Finance, HR, IT, and Legal.

action: Detected: Expense Policy vs Procurement Limits
Validating Expense Policy vs Procurement Limits

citations.passage: Managers hold discretionary approval authority for operational expenses up to $10,000.
All software purchases exceeding $500 must undergo IT Security review and formal procurement. No exceptions.

conclusion: Validated strict contradiction regarding Expense Policy vs Procurement Limits.

conclusion.summary: Reconcile approval limits. Route software >$500 through IT procurement.
conclusion.recommendation: Update Expense Policy Section 4 to explicitly carve out software and refer to Procurement Agreement.

conflict_record.title: Expense Policy vs Procurement Limits
conflict_record.description: Global Expense Policy allows managers to approve up to $10,000 in software purchases, but the Procurement Master Agreement strictly limits unvetted software purchases to $500 to prevent shadow IT.

resolution.summary: Reconcile approval limits. Route software >$500 through IT procurement.
resolution.recommendation: Update Expense Policy Section 4 to explicitly carve out software and refer to Procurement Agreement.

evidence.passage: Managers hold discretionary approval authority for operational expenses up to $10,000.
All software purchases exceeding $500 must undergo IT Security review and formal procurement. No exceptions.

action: Detected: Data Retention vs Remote Work Storage
Validating Data Retention vs Remote Work Storage

citations.passage: Customer PII must be irrecoverably purged after 3 years.
Employees may maintain local offline copies of necessary files to ensure productivity during outages.

conclusion: Validated strict contradiction regarding Data Retention vs Remote Work Storage.

potential_consequences: Stale data leaks from stolen laptops

conclusion.summary: Ban permanent local backups of customer data.
conclusion.recommendation: Update Remote Work Guidelines to enforce 30-day auto-wipes on local offline customer data.

conflict_record.title: Data Retention vs Remote Work Storage
conflict_record.description: Data Retention Policy requires all customer data to be purged after 3 years, but Remote Work Guidelines allow employees to use local offline backups indefinitely.

resolution.summary: Ban permanent local backups of customer data.
resolution.recommendation: Update Remote Work Guidelines to enforce 30-day auto-wipes on local offline customer data.

potential_consequences: Stale data leaks from stolen laptops

evidence.passage: Customer PII must be irrecoverably purged after 3 years.
Employees may maintain local offline copies of necessary files to ensure productivity during outages.

action: Detected: Contractor Access vs Zero Trust Mandate
Validating Contractor Access vs Zero Trust Mandate

citations.passage: Vendors will be issued standard VPN profiles for internal network access.
External entities shall never be granted VPN access. All access must be app-specific via the Zero Trust gateway.

conclusion: Validated strict contradiction regarding Contractor Access vs Zero Trust Mandate.

conclusion.recommendation: Migrate all vendors to Zero Trust Application Access.

conflict_record.title: Contractor Access vs Zero Trust Mandate
conflict_record.description: Vendor Management Policy grants vendors VPN access, but Zero Trust Architecture restricts all external access to specific application proxies.

resolution.recommendation: Migrate all vendors to Zero Trust Application Access.

evidence.passage: Vendors will be issued standard VPN profiles for internal network access.
External entities shall never be granted VPN access. All access must be app-specific via the Zero Trust gateway.

conclusion.summary: Enterprise Audit detected multiple high-risk compliance gaps across Finance, IT, and Legal.
conclusion.recommendation: Immediate attention required from CFO and CISO.
